description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a blue crab, featuring a broad, flattened carapace with a slightly rough texture and serrated edges. The crab has two prominent claws (chelae) that are tinged with a reddish-pink hue at the tips, contrasting with the blue and gray coloration of the legs and body. Its five pairs of legs are splayed outward, with the last pair being paddle-shaped, characteristic of swimming crabs. The carapace is mottled with subtle color variations, and the underside is not visible in these views. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front corners of the carapace, and no antennae are clearly visible. The overall form is compact and low to the ground, typical of crabs found in coastal or urban seafood markets.
description: A blue crab with a broad, rough-edged carapace, blue-gray legs, and reddish-tipped claws, featuring eyes on short stalks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's wide carapace, both claws extended forward, and eyes on short stalks.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the left claw, the segmentation of the legs, and the paddle-shaped rear leg.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full span of the legs and the symmetrical shape of the carapace.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the right, emphasizing the right claw and the curvature of the legs.
